opp.ais_why:
• No Avoma meetings linked to this opp or to the LADOTD account — transcript review turned up nothing.
• Rick Taranek's 5/13 next step describes a slow public-sector RFI: 'Prospect not sharing results of RFI, competitive field of vendors. Slow mover.' No AI positioning anywhere.
• SF Description is null and the cadence reads as a slow RFI with no Merlin or agentic language — AI mandate is absent.

items.area: Buyer not sharing RFI results or shortlist status despite rep outreach 17 Jun; Zycus may be sidelined in a compliance-driven process or the Director of Procurement lacks authority/willingness to disclose evaluation details. Risk of silent disqualification or losing to a better-connected incumbent.
items.quote: Julie/Prospect not sharing results of RFI. Sent reminder again today.

items.area: Zero contact roles on the opportunity; single-threaded to one Director of Procurement; no EB, no champion, no CIO/CFO/Secretary-level sponsor mapped. Government deals require multi-level buy-in; lack of executive alignment is a gate-blocking risk.

Deal in Qualified stage for 151 days with Oct 2026 close date (117 days out); no progression to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted despite RFI submission. If RFI results and next gates are not confirmed within ~30 days, the Oct close is not credible.

No discovery, demo, or value-case validation; buyer's pain points, ROI expectations, and product-fit requirements entirely unconfirmed. Zycus cannot differentiate or tailor its proposal without a two-way discovery conversation.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI target has been captured. The opportunity record contains no value-to-customer, business-objectives, or compelling-event fields populated (all Salesforce custom fields queried returned null). No discovery or demo calls were discovered in the Avoma opp+account pull, so there is no buyer-articulated pain, cost burden, cycle-time improvement, or savings expectation on record. The product scope is broad (ten modules including Merlin AI and full procure-to-pay suite per Products field), but without a quantified pain point Zycus cannot tailor ROI messaging or justify the investment to government budget approvers. A government deal of this size typically requires a documented cost-benefit analysis or legislative justification; none exists. The highest-leverage move is to secure a discovery session with Julie Kennison and the budget authority (likely the CFO or Secretary of Transportation office) to quantify current-state inefficiency costs (manual invoice processing, supplier onboarding cycle time, maverick spend) and build a defensible ROI model anchored to their FY budget cycle.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query: zero roles, including no Champion role
Next Step 2026-06-17: Julie Kennison 'not sharing results of RFI', rep sent reminder (no response implied)
Salesforce LastActivityDate 2026-06-18 (16 days ago, rep-initiated per Next Step)
Salesforce X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Champion_Identified__c (both null)
Avoma opp+account discovery: zero matched buyer calls, no evidence of advocacy or introductions
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. The Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query returned zero contact roles, so there is no Champion role formally assigned. The only named prospect contact is Julie Kennison (Director of Procurement), referenced in the Next Step (2026-06-17) and task history. However, Julie is withholding RFI results and not responding to rep outreach per the Next Step ('not sharing results of RFI. Sent reminder again today'), which is the opposite of champion behavior. A true champion would advocate internally for Zycus, share competitive intelligence, introduce the rep to the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, and drive the evaluation timeline forward. Julie's silence and deflection may indicate she is a gatekeeper or compliance coordinator with no decision authority or personal stake in the outcome, or that Zycus is no longer in the running and she is avoiding an uncomfortable conversation. There is no evidence of access to power (no EB or DM mapped), no evidence of advocacy (no introductions, no internal documents shared, no competitive de-positioning), and engagement is cooling (16 days since last activity, rep-initiated only). The deal is effectively single-threaded to a contact who may not be a champion at all. The highest-leverage move is to test Julie's willingness to champion by asking her directly for an introduction to the budget approver and the RFI results; if she declines or continues to delay, Zycus must bypass her and reach the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker through the deal owner's manager, a partner relationship, or a public-sector executive referral. Without a champion, this deal will not close.

competition.narrative: Competition is entirely unknown. The Salesforce Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c fields are both null. The Next Step (2026-06-17) references an RFI submission, which in government procurement typically means multiple vendors were invited to respond, but no competitor is named. The Salesforce How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, and Replacing_What__c fields are null, so whether Louisiana DOTD has an incumbent procurement system or manual/legacy process is unconfirmed. No discovery or demo calls were found in Avoma, so there is no buyer mention of competitor evaluation, no shortlist visibility, and no competitive wedge discussed. Government RFIs often attract a known cohort of public-sector vendors: Jaggaer, Bonfire, Oracle, SAP Ariba, Coupa (if state purchasing cooperative pricing exists), or regional government-focused resellers. Without knowing who else responded, Zycus cannot differentiate on price, functionality, or government reference strength, and cannot tailor its next-move messaging to counter a likely competitor's positioning. The buyer's withholding of RFI results (per Next Step 2026-06-17) suggests either a formal blackout period (common in government procurements to ensure fairness) or that Zycus is not advancing and the buyer is avoiding the conversation. The move is to ask Julie Kennison directly who else is being evaluated and what the shortlist status is; if she will not disclose, escalate to request a formal RFI debrief (standard practice in many state procurement codes) or reach a higher contact through the deal owner's manager who can share competitive context.

sources: Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c (all null)
Avoma opp+account discovery: zero matched buyer calls
Salesforce Products__c: ten-module scope implies transformation intent, but pain unconfirmed
Next Step 2026-06-17: RFI referenced but no pain or urgency stated
identify_pain.narrative: The buyer's operational and financial pain is entirely unconfirmed. The Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Compelling_Event__c fields are all null. No discovery or demo calls were found in the Avoma opp+account pull, so there is no buyer-articulated pain statement on record. The product scope (ten modules: iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA per Products field) implies a large-scale procurement transformation, but the triggering pain is unknown. Common government procurement pain points include manual invoice processing (high FTE cost, payment delays, audit risk), fragmented supplier onboarding (long cycle times, compliance gaps), lack of spend visibility (maverick buying, budget overruns), and paper-based contract management (version control failures, expired-contract risk). Whether Louisiana DOTD suffers from any of these, and which pain is severe enough to justify a $150k SaaS investment and a multi-year implementation, is unconfirmed. The lack of a compelling event is a critical gap: government deals rarely close without a legislated mandate, a budget deadline, an audit finding, or a contract expiration forcing action. The immediate move is to conduct a discovery call with Julie Kennison and ideally a business owner (e.g., Accounts Payable director, Contracts manager) to surface and quantify the current-state pain, asking 'What happens if you do not solve this by Q4 2026?' to uncover the urgency driver.

sources: Salesforce Current_Contract_Expiration__c, Mandate__c (both null)
Next Step 2026-06-17: no mention of contracting, legal, or approval mechanics
Avoma opp+account discovery: zero matched buyer calls, no SOW or MSA discussion
Salesforce Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c (both null)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting and procurement mechanics are entirely unknown. The Salesforce Current_Contract_Expiration__c field is null, so there is no incumbent contract expiration forcing function documented. State government procurements typically follow strict purchasing statutes (e.g., Louisiana Procurement Code), often requiring multi-vendor competition, public bid openings, and legislative or controller approval for contracts above a threshold (commonly $50k–$100k). Whether this deal follows a formal RFP statute, a cooperative purchasing vehicle (e.g., Sourcewell, NASPO), or an exception process is unconfirmed. InfoSec review, ERP integration scope (Louisiana DOTD likely runs an ERP such as Oracle or SAP for financial systems), MSA/SOW templates, and legal redline cycles are not discussed in any field, task, or call. The $150k amount may trigger additional oversight (e.g., Joint Legislative Committee on the Budget review in Louisiana). Without visibility into the paper process, Zycus cannot forecast contracting duration or prepare the required compliance documentation. The move is to ask Julie Kennison or the LADOTD legal/procurement office what the contract approval path is, what templates and terms apply, and whether a formal RFP will follow the RFI, securing this in writing to anchor the timeline.

sources: Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query: zero roles returned
Next Step 2026-06-17: Julie Kennison (Director of Procurement) is sole named contact, 'not sharing results of RFI'
Salesforce X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c (all null)
Avoma opp+account discovery: zero matched buyer calls, no executive-level attendees
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. The Salesforce OpportunityContactRole query returned zero contact roles, so no EB, Executive Sponsor, or CFO-level contact is formally mapped. The only named prospect contact across all evidence is Julie Kennison, identified as Director of Procurement in task descriptions. In a state government agency, procurement directors typically coordinate vendor evaluations but do not control budget approval for six-figure SaaS investments; that authority sits with the CFO, the Secretary of Transportation, or the state CIO office depending on Louisiana's procurement governance model. No calls, tasks, or activities reference any executive-level participant. The Next Step (2026-06-17) describes Julie as withholding RFI results, which may signal limited authority or a compliance-driven process where she is not the decision-maker. Without EB identification and engagement, Zycus cannot align commercial terms, validate budget availability, or secure executive sponsorship required to close a government deal. The immediate move is to ask Julie directly who will approve the contract and budget, and to request an introduction to that authority, escalating through the deal owner's manager if Julie continues to deflect.

sources: Next Step 2026-06-17: 'Julie/Prospect not sharing results of RFI. Sent reminder again today.'
Salesforce What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c (all null)
Avoma opp+account discovery: zero matched buyer calls, no process discussion
OpportunityContactRole query: zero roles, implying no multi-level engagement
decision_process.narrative: The process is partially visible but not confirmed with the buyer. The Next Step (2026-06-17) confirms an RFI round has occurred and Zycus submitted a response, which is consistent with a formal government procurement process (typically: RFI → shortlist → RFP → vendor selection → contract negotiation). However, the buyer is withholding the RFI results and shortlist status per the same Next Step, so Zycus does not know whether it advanced or where the process stands today. The Salesforce Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c and Multiple_approvals__c fields are null, so the internal approval chain (e.g., procurement review → CIO endorsement → CFO budget sign-off → Secretary or Commissioner final approval) is unconfirmed. Government deals often involve multi-tier review boards, legislative budget committee oversight, and public records compliance; none of this is documented. The lack of a second contact role or executive engagement suggests Zycus has not mapped the approval ladder. The process risk is silent disqualification: if Zycus was not shortlisted and the buyer is now in RFP or finalist demos with other vendors, the October close is lost. The immediate move is to demand clarity on RFI results and next-gate timing, escalating to the deal owner's manager if Julie Kennison does not respond within 7 days.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria have not been documented. The Salesforce Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c fields are all null. The Next Step references an RFI submission, which implies Louisiana DOTD issued a formal request and Zycus responded, but the RFI document itself and the buyer's weighted scoring model are not captured in Salesforce or discussed in any call (zero discovery/demo calls found). The product scope (ten modules including Merlin AI) suggests a comprehensive procurement transformation, but without the buyer's stated priorities—such as compliance reporting, supplier diversity tracking, invoice automation ROI, or AI-driven sourcing—Zycus cannot tailor its demo, pricing, or implementation plan to the criteria that will determine shortlisting and selection. Government RFIs typically publish weighted evaluation factors (technical capability, cost, implementation timeline, incumbent-agency references); these must be on file somewhere. The move is to request a debrief on the RFI evaluation criteria directly from Julie Kennison, or if she continues to withhold information, to escalate through the deal owner's manager to a higher LADOTD contact who can share the RFI scoring framework and current vendor standing.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AI appetite is unknown. No AIS fields populated (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null), and no discovery or demo calls where AI needs or interest were discussed. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake, suggesting Zycus proposed AI modules in the RFI, but buyer reaction and prioritization are unconfirmed. Government agencies vary widely in AI maturity; some are mandated to explore automation (e.g., federal AI EO, state digital transformation initiatives), others are risk-averse and prefer proven manual workflows. Without a two-way conversation, tier is unassignable.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — AI appetite is unknown.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning is entirely untested. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake (AI modules per Products field), but no AIS score, status, or rationale is recorded (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null). No discovery or demo calls were found, so Zycus has not presented the Merlin negotiation agent, intake automation, or agentic procurement story to Louisiana DOTD. Whether the buyer has AI appetite, whether the RFI included AI/automation requirements, and whether AI differentiation would matter in the shortlist decision are all unknown. Government buyers often prioritize compliance, audit trail, and cost over innovation, so AI may not be a decision driver unless framed as cost savings (FTE reduction, faster cycle time). Without a conversation, Zycus cannot position Merlin as a competitive wedge.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Louisiana Department Of Transportation and Development — Qualified · $150K · closes 29 Oct

contributions.evidence: Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 28 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent; Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 4 is carried by momentum 50 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.93).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 37 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 1 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (4), nudged by commitment 37 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 28. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

items.commitment: Share RFI evaluation results and vendor shortlist status with Zycus

items.action: Secure RFI debrief and shortlist status confirmation from Julie Kennison within 7 days. If she does not respond by 2026-07-11, escalate through the deal owner's manager to reach the Economic Buyer (likely the CFO or Secretary of Transportation office) or the state CIO to determine whether Zycus is still in the running and what the next evaluation gate and timeline are. Frame as 'We want to ensure we are aligned with your timeline and can provide any additional information LADOTD needs to make a confident decision.'
items.trigger: Buyer withholding RFI results for 17+ days despite rep reminder 2026-06-17; no response, no scheduled next step, and Oct 2026 close requires Shortlisted decision within ~30 days to be credible.
items.expected_effect: Clarifies whether Zycus advanced to shortlist and unlocks next-gate visibility; if not advancing, allows immediate re-qualification or closure; if advancing, re-engages buyer and schedules demo or finalist presentation. Without this, deal remains dark and Oct close is unachievable.

items.action: Executive connect: the deal owner's manager reaches the Economic Buyer or a C-level sponsor (CFO, Secretary of Transportation, or state CIO) to introduce Zycus at the executive level, validate budget availability and approval authority, and request a discovery session to align Zycus solution with LADOTD's strategic procurement transformation goals. Position as 'We work with many state DOTs on procurement modernization and would value the opportunity to understand your priorities and share relevant peer outcomes.'
items.trigger: Zero Contact Roles, no EB or DM identified, and 151 days in Qualified with no executive engagement. Government deals require C-level sponsorship to close; single-threading to a Director of Procurement who is not responding will not advance the deal.
items.expected_effect: Maps the Economic Buyer, validates budget and decision authority, opens executive-level dialogue, and bypasses the procurement gatekeeper. Increases visibility into decision process and builds multi-level relationship required to close a government deal.

items.action: Conduct discovery and ROI quantification session with Julie Kennison and ideally a business owner (Accounts Payable director, Contracts manager, or IT lead) to surface and quantify current-state pain: manual invoice processing costs, supplier onboarding cycle time, maverick spend visibility gaps, contract expiration risks. Ask 'What operational or financial problem is driving this procurement, and what happens if it is not solved by Q4 2026?' Build a documented ROI model (FTE savings, cycle-time reduction, error-rate improvement) anchored to their FY budget cycle and legislative justification requirements.
items.trigger: No discovery or demo calls on record; buyer pain, compelling event, and quantified value case entirely unconfirmed per Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c (all null) and zero Avoma buyer calls. Without a validated pain and ROI case, Zycus cannot differentiate or justify investment to government budget approvers.
items.expected_effect: Uncovers the true pain and urgency driver, quantifies ROI to support CFO/budget approval, and validates product-fit alignment. Discovery session also tests Julie Kennison's engagement and willingness to advocate; if she declines or delays, confirms she is not a champion and escalation is required.

items.action: Request formal RFI scoring criteria and Zycus evaluation feedback from Julie Kennison or the LADOTD procurement office. Many state procurement codes allow or require vendor debriefs on RFI evaluations. Use this to understand how Zycus scored on technical capability, cost, implementation timeline, and government references, and where gaps exist. If Julie will not provide, escalate through the deal owner's manager to request debrief as a standard procurement practice.
items.trigger: RFI submission referenced in Next Step 2026-06-17 but evaluation criteria, scoring model, and Zycus standing are unknown per Salesforce Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c (null) and zero calls discussing criteria. Without this, Zycus cannot tailor demo, pricing, or proposal to the weighted factors that determine shortlist advancement.
items.expected_effect: Reveals the buyer's weighted decision criteria, Zycus competitive standing, and any gaps or objections that must be addressed in the next round. If Zycus scored low on mandatory requirements, allows course-correction or honest re-qualification; if Zycus scored well, provides competitive wedge to emphasize in finalist demos.

items.action: Identify and engage a government procurement reference or partner who has a relationship with Louisiana DOTD or Louisiana state procurement leadership to provide a warm introduction, validate Zycus credibility with a peer testimonial, and share competitive intelligence on the RFI shortlist. Alternatively, invite LADOTD to a peer roundtable or Horizon event where other state DOT procurement leaders discuss their Zycus implementations and ROI outcomes.
items.trigger: Single-threaded to one non-responsive contact, no executive access, and buyer withholding RFI results suggest lack of trust or relationship depth. Government buyers value peer references and are influenced by other agencies' successes. Competition is unknown, and a better-connected incumbent or rival with strong Louisiana relationships may be winning.
items.expected_effect: Builds credibility and trust through peer validation, bypasses the procurement gatekeeper to reach decision-makers, and surfaces competitive intelligence. A warm introduction from a trusted peer (e.g., another state DOT CFO or CIO) can re-open a stalled conversation and accelerate shortlist confirmation.

items: Last activity 16 days ago (2026-06-18), rep-initiated outreach only per Next Step 2026-06-17

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling and confidence is low. Last activity 16 days ago (2026-06-18), rep-initiated only (reminder sent 2026-06-17 per Next Step). Buyer (Julie Kennison) not responding or sharing RFI results. Zero two-way buyer engagement: no calls, no meetings, no inbound emails or tasks logged. Single-threaded to one contact with no executive or multi-level engagement. Deal has been in Qualified for 151 days with no stage movement, no scheduled next gate, and no validated pipeline activity. Risk of silent disqualification or that buyer is in advanced stages with other vendors while Zycus is held at arm's length.

north_star_verdict.math: 117 days to close (2026-10-29). In Qualified stage since creation 2026-02-03 (151 days in stage). No progression to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted despite RFI submission. Required pace to close: must reach Shortlisted within ~30 days, Vendor Selected within ~60 days, and Negotiation within ~90 days to support an Oct close. Current velocity: zero stage movement in 151 days. Forward slip risk is high: no buyer validation of RFI standing, no scheduled next gate. Time-in-Qualified alone (151d) exceeds the typical enterprise discovery-to-RFI window (90-120d), signaling either stall or silent disqualification.
evidence: Stage: Qualified since 2026-02-03 (151 days), per Salesforce StageName and CreatedDate

RFI submission referenced in Next Step but buyer 'not sharing results' per 2026-06-17 Next Step
No EB, DM, or Champion Contact Roles; no executive-level engagement in any activity
north_star_verdict.headline: Single-threaded government deal stalled in Qualified for 143 days with buyer withholding RFI results; no EB/DM mapped, no discovery calls, and Oct 2026 close requires immediate multi-level engagement or re-qualification.

flags: RFI blackout: buyer not sharing evaluation results or shortlist status despite 17 Jun outreach; Zycus is being kept at arm's length in a compliance-driven process, signaling weak relationship or possible silent disqualification risk. The deal owner must escalate internally (to the owner's manager for executive air cover) and externally (to a higher buyer stakeholder, e.g. Chief Procurement Officer or CIO) to break the silence and confirm Zycus remains in consideration.
Single-threaded: only one prospect contact (Julie Kennison) across all evidence; zero Contact Roles; no multi-threading to EB, DM, or business owner
No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified or engaged; government deal cannot close without CFO/Secretary-level budget authority mapped and sponsoring
No discovery or demo calls; buyer pain, ROI case, and product fit entirely unvalidated
Stalled in Qualified for 151 days with no stage progression despite RFI submission; typical enterprise Qualified-to-Shortlisted window is 60-90 days
Buyer withholding RFI results and shortlist status for 17+ days despite rep outreach; risk of silent disqualification or process blackout
No champion; Julie Kennison not responding or advocating, engagement is rep-initiated only
Competition unknown; no shortlist visibility, no competitive positioning strategy
Close date 117 days out (Oct 2026) requires Shortlisted within ~30 days and Vendor Selected within ~60 days; current velocity (zero stage movement in 151d) makes this timeline indefensible
best_practice_check.summary: Critical best-practice violations: single-threaded (one contact, no Contact Roles), no EB/DM mapped, no discovery calls, 151 days in Qualified with no stage movement, buyer withholding RFI results, and close date 117 days out with no validated next gate. This deal requires immediate multi-level re-engagement or re-qualification.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown. No competitor is named in Salesforce (Competitors__c null) or in any activity. The Next Step references an RFI submission, which in government procurement implies a multi-vendor process, but the buyer is withholding RFI results and shortlist status. Without competitive intelligence, Zycus cannot differentiate or tailor its positioning. The lack of discovery calls and the buyer's silence suggest either a formal procurement blackout period or that Zycus is not advancing and the buyer is avoiding disclosure. Immediate move: demand RFI debrief and shortlist visibility, escalating if necessary.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is entirely untested. The product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake (AI modules per Products field), but no AIS score, status, or rationale is recorded (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null). No discovery or demo calls were found, so Zycus has not presented the Merlin negotiation agent, intake automation, or agentic procurement story to Louisiana DOTD. Whether the buyer has AI appetite, whether the RFI included AI/automation requirements, and whether AI differentiation would matter in the shortlist decision are all unknown. Government buyers often prioritize compliance, audit trail, and cost over innovation, so AI may not be a decision driver unless framed as cost savings (FTE reduction, faster cycle time). Without a conversation, Zycus cannot position Merlin as a competitive wedge.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is entirely unvalidated. No discovery or demo calls were found, and no business requirements, decision criteria, or pain points are documented in Salesforce. The product scope is broad (ten modules including Merlin AI and full P2P suite), but whether Louisiana DOTD's priorities align with Zycus strengths (e.g., AI-driven sourcing, supplier risk, contract lifecycle automation) is unknown. The buyer's withholding of RFI results may signal misalignment or that Zycus did not meet mandatory requirements. Without a two-way conversation, Zycus is flying blind on fit.

hard.ais_why:
• No Avoma meetings linked to this opp or to the LADOTD account — transcript review turned up nothing.
• Rick Taranek's 5/13 next step describes a slow public-sector RFI: 'Prospect not sharing results of RFI, competitive field of vendors. Slow mover.' No AI positioning anywhere.
• SF Description is null and the cadence reads as a slow RFI with no Merlin or agentic language — AI mandate is absent.

deltas.subject: Single-threaded to a Director of Procurement with zero other buyer-side contacts mapped; no champion, no EB, no CIO/CFO/Secretary-level sponsor. Government deals require multi-level buy-in; relying on one contact who is not sharing information is a ghost risk. Multi-thread immediately by requesting introductions to the evaluation committee, the budget holder, and IT/InfoSec leads.

Zero discovery or demo calls in ~143 days; no quantified value case, no pain articulation, no product fit validation. Without a two-way conversation that surfaces the buyer's operational pain, fiscal constraints, and evaluation priorities, Zycus cannot differentiate, tailor its proposal, or build urgency. The next action must be a discovery session (ideally with multiple buyer stakeholders) that co-builds the business case and positions Merlin AI as a state-government procurement automation breakthrough.

champion

gaps: Zero buyer calls discovered in Avoma opp+account pull (both opportunity-linked and account-linked meetings queried, account domain dotd.la.gov used for attendee matching); no discovery, demo, or validation sessions on record. This is a knowledge gap: buyer pain, product fit, evaluation criteria, competitive standing, and stakeholder sentiment are entirely unconfirmed.

Zero Contact Roles on the opportunity per OpportunityContactRole query; no EB, DM, Champion, or any role formally mapped. Single-threaded to Julie Kennison (Director of Procurement) per Next Step and task history. This is a knowledge gap: Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, and champion are unidentified.

All MEDDPICC and competitive custom fields (Competitors__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Business_Requirements__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c, and others) returned null in Q2/Q3 SOQL queries. This is a knowledge gap: metrics, pain, EB, decision criteria, decision process, paper process, and competition are all unconfirmed.
No AIS fields populated (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c all null). AI appetite and fit are unknown. This is a knowledge gap.
Buyer withholding RFI evaluation results and shortlist status per Next Step 2026-06-17; no visibility into whether Zycus advanced or who else is being evaluated. This is a knowledge gap.
